title: ricinocarpus leptorhachis
side_effects: Ricinocarpus leptorhachis can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing severe gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its toxic alkaloid compounds. Ingesting large quantities can lead to cardiovascular problems, including hypertension and arrhythmias, as well as renal impairment. Prolonged exposure to the plant's toxins may also cause neurological symptoms, including dizziness, tremors, and in severe cases, seizures and coma. Misuse of the plant can result in long-term health consequences, including liver damage and immune system suppression.
